# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: v1   # Define API version. This should match with your setup - kube-apiserver and etcd versions are used here for simplicity purpose as different releases can have slightly differing behavior/capabilities than the other one we chose above e.g., Kubernetes 2 vs EKS
kind: ConfigMap   # Define resource kind, in this case is a configmap to hold Kong configuration details which will be loaded by kube-proxy at runtime and used as an init container when deploying services onto cluster like Ingress Nginx/Traefik. If you need other types of resources then use appropriate Kubernetes constructs (Deployment, DaemonSet)
metadata:  # Define metadata for resource - This is required to be present regardless if the configmap should store information or not by kube-proxy etc., usually used in place where data must persist beyond a single pod restart. In case of Kong's configuration it might just point around '/etc/kong'.
   name: kong-config  # Define metadata field - This is required to be present on every resource file you write and defines the unique identifier for that particular configmap entry which can then act as an index during runtime. It should match with how Kubernetes reads from this ConfigMap in real time e.g., 'kubelet'
   namespace: kong  # Define metadata field - This is required to be present on every resource file you write and defines the namespaces for which your data will live within, ie.: where Kong should store it or Kubernetes context if we are in a multi-cluster scenario. For simplicity here assumed value of 'kong'.
data:  # Define section used while defining Data - It's an object that holds key/value pairs and acts as the data for configmaps within kubelet (i.e., runtime environment) to allow dynamically loading Kong configurations into Kubernetes when service is being created on cluster using Ingress Nginx Controller or similar tools etc..
   proxy_conf: | # Data section of ConfigMap can hold arbitrary configuration that's sent in string format and will be available for the 'kubelet'. This allows easy inclusion/exclusion based upon specific scenarios. The following is an example which could include Kong configurations details about proxying to microservices, setting up services (e.g., hosts), routes etc
     proxy_conf: |
       http://my-api1.example.com api/v2 myserviceA  # define the target service's url and specific API prefix or path if required by your backend APIs e.g.(http://myservicea.foo.bar, /apis/, etc.)
